user_story: As an operator, I want to display the CLI's current configuration so that I can confirm which API server and credentials it will use.
expected_behavior: Prints the resolved API URL and whether an API key is set (green 'Set' / yellow 'Not set'), plus the raw CMDB_API_URL and CMDB_API_KEY environment variable states. Makes no network call. The apiUrl defaults to http://localhost:3000/api (index.ts:28) and is overridable via -u/--url or CMDB_API_URL; -k/--key or CMDB_API_KEY supplies the bearer token.

user_story: As an operator, I want to check that the API server is reachable and healthy so that I know the platform is up before running commands.
expected_behavior: Issues GET {apiUrl}/health with a 5s timeout and on success prints status, version and uptime. On failure it fails the spinner and prints tailored messages for ECONNREFUSED and ETIMEDOUT, then exits with code 1. The default base URL plus path resolve to http://localhost:3000/api/health.
issue_note: Default base is http://localhost:3000/api (index.ts:28) so the path resolves to /api/health, but the server exposes health only at /api/v1/cmdb-health (server.ts:82); the check always reports unreachable. This /api-vs-/api/v1 base-path gap affects every REST-backed CLI command unless CMDB_API_URL is overridden to include /v1.

user_story: As an engineer, I want to list configuration items with type/status filters and pagination so that I can browse the inventory.
expected_behavior: Sends GET /cis with params _limit, _offset and optional type/status, then prints the reported total and each item's name, id, type, colorized status and description. The server's queryFiltersSchema only recognizes limit/offset (plain), so the underscored _limit/_offset params are stripped (validators.ts stripUnknown) and the --limit/--offset flags are silently ignored; type/status do bind.

user_story: As an engineer, I want to fetch a single CI's details, optionally with relationships, so that I can inspect it.
expected_behavior: Sends GET /cis/:id (adding includeRelationships=true when --relationships is passed) and prints id, name, type, colorized status, description, attributes map, inbound/outbound relationship counts, and created/updated timestamps. Errors are surfaced via handleError showing the API message and status.

user_story: As an engineer, I want to create a CI from the command line so that I can register new infrastructure.
expected_behavior: Requires -t/--type and -n/--name, parses optional --attributes JSON (failing fast on invalid JSON), POSTs to /cis, and prints the new id/name/type. The body it sends is {_type, _name, description?, attributes?}.
issue_note: ciInputSchema (validators.ts:123) requires plain id, name and type; the CLI sends underscored _type/_name and omits the required id, so after stripUnknown all keys drop and the request 400s. It also sends description/attributes which the schema does not define (it uses metadata).

user_story: As an engineer, I want to update a CI's name/description/status/attributes so that I can keep records current.
expected_behavior: Builds a partial payload from --name/--description/--status/--attributes (validating attributes JSON), aborts if no fields given, then issues PATCH /cis/:id and reports success.
issue_note: The CLI uses HTTP PATCH but ci.routes.ts:64 defines only PUT /cis/:id, so PATCH is unrouted and returns 404. Additionally description/attributes are not in updateCISchema (name/type/status/environment/metadata) and would be stripped even if the verb matched.

user_story: As an engineer, I want to delete a CI with a confirmation guard so that I do not remove records accidentally.
expected_behavior: Without -f/--force it prints a warning and returns without deleting. With --force it sends DELETE /cis/:id and reports success, surfacing API errors via handleError.

user_story: As an engineer, I want to create a relationship between two CIs so that I can model dependencies.
expected_behavior: Requires --from, --to and --type, POSTs to /relationships, and prints the new relationship id plus the from/to/type echo. The body sent is {_fromCiId, _toCiId, _type}.
issue_note: relationshipSchema (validators.ts:137) requires plain from_id, to_id and type; the CLI's underscored _fromCiId/_toCiId/_type are stripped by stripUnknown, leaving the required fields missing so the request 400s.

user_story: As an engineer, I want to list a CI's inbound/outbound relationships so that I can understand its connections.
expected_behavior: Sends GET /cis/:id/relationships?direction=<value> and renders inbound (fromCi.name --[type]--> ciId) and outbound (ciId --[type]--> toCi.name) edges, printing 'No relationships found' when empty. Default direction 'both' is accepted by the server.
issue_note: The --direction option advertises inbound/outbound/both, but the route's relationshipQuerySchema (ci.routes.ts:31-33) only allows in/out/both; passing --direction inbound or outbound fails validation with 400.

user_story: As an engineer, I want to search CIs by name/attributes so that I can quickly find items.
expected_behavior: Sends a GET to /cis/search with query, _limit and optional type as query-string params and prints matching name/id/type/status, expecting an array response (reads response.data.length).
issue_note: Search is defined as POST /cis/search with a JSON body {query, limit} (ci.routes.ts:47). A GET to /cis/search instead matches the GET /cis/:id route with id='search' (controller.getCIById), so it never reaches searchCIs and returns a wrong/404 result; the limit is also sent as _limit, not limit.

user_story: As an analyst, I want to execute an ad-hoc query string so that I can retrieve CMDB data in json/table/csv.
expected_behavior: POSTs {_query, _limit} to /query/execute and formats the returned array as table (default), json, or csv via formatOutput. Aliased as 'q'.
issue_note: No /query routes are mounted in the REST server (server.ts setupRoutes has no /api/v1/query); every command in the query group targets a non-existent /query/* surface and 404s. The whole 'query' group is unreachable against the real API.

user_story: As an analyst, I want to find CIs matching attribute filters with AND/OR so that I can target subsets.
expected_behavior: Collects repeated -a key=value flags into an attributes map plus optional type and --operator (AND/OR), POSTs {filters, _limit} to /query/find, and prints matching name/id/type/status.
issue_note: Part of the unmounted /query group (no /query route in server.ts; see CLI-011) so it 404s in practice.

user_story: As an analyst, I want the full dependency chain of a CI so that I can see what it relies on.
expected_behavior: Sends GET /query/dependencies/:id with params _depth, _direction (upstream/downstream/both) and optional relationshipType, then prints a recursive dependency tree.
issue_note: No /query route is mounted; the actual dependency endpoint is GET /cis/:id/dependencies (ci.routes.ts:81), so the CLI points at the wrong, non-existent path and 404s.

user_story: As an analyst, I want to analyze the impact of a CI failing so that I can assess blast radius.
expected_behavior: Sends GET /query/impact/:id with depth, then prints either raw JSON (--format json) or an impact tree showing root name, affected count, and critical/affected CIs.
issue_note: No /query route is mounted; the real impact endpoint is GET /cis/:id/impact (ci.routes.ts:88). The CLI targets a non-existent /query/impact path and 404s.

user_story: As an analyst, I want to query infrastructure topology so that I can see nodes and edges by type.
expected_behavior: Sends GET /query/topology with params _depth and optional root/type, then prints total node and edge counts plus a per-type node tally derived from response.data.nodes.
issue_note: Part of the unmounted /query group (see CLI-011); 404s in practice.

user_story: As an analyst, I want to list my saved queries so that I can reuse them.
expected_behavior: Sends GET /query/saved and prints each saved query's name, description and created timestamp, or 'No saved queries' when the array is empty.
issue_note: Part of the unmounted /query group (see CLI-011); 404s in practice.

user_story: As an analyst, I want to save a query under a name so that I can run it later.
expected_behavior: POSTs {name, _query, _description} to /query/saved and confirms the query was saved.
issue_note: Part of the unmounted /query group (see CLI-011); 404s in practice.

user_story: As an analyst, I want to execute a saved query by name so that I can repeat analyses.
expected_behavior: Sends GET /query/saved/:name/execute and formats the returned array via formatOutput in the chosen --format (table default).
issue_note: Part of the unmounted /query group (see CLI-011); 404s in practice.

user_story: As an operator, I want to start a discovery scan against targets so that new CIs are discovered.
expected_behavior: Parses --targets (comma list) and optional SSH credentials, POSTs {_targets, _scanType, credentials?} to /discovery/scan, then prints the returned scanId and a follow-up status hint. Errors if neither --targets nor --file is given. Aliased as 'disc'.
issue_note: There is no POST /discovery/scan route; discovery.routes.ts only exposes /schedule and /jobs(/:id), so the call 404s. Separately, the --file target source is an explicit stub: 'File input not yet implemented' (discovery.command.ts:104-106).

user_story: As an operator, I want to list discovery scans with optional status filter so that I can review past runs.
expected_behavior: Sends GET /discovery/scans with params _limit and optional status, then prints each scan's id, colorized status, type, target count and started/completed timestamps.
issue_note: The discovery routes expose listing at GET /discovery/jobs (discovery.routes.ts:54), not /discovery/scans, so the request 404s; _limit/status would also be stripped versus the underscored listJobsQuerySchema.

user_story: As an operator, I want the status of a specific scan so that I can monitor progress.
expected_behavior: Sends GET /discovery/scans/:id and prints id, status, type, target count, discovered count, timestamps, computed duration, and a progress line when present.
issue_note: Single-job status is served at GET /discovery/jobs/:id (discovery.routes.ts:48), not /discovery/scans/:id, so the call 404s.

user_story: As an operator, I want to cancel a running scan so that I can stop unwanted discovery.
expected_behavior: Sends POST /discovery/scans/:id/cancel with an empty body and reports the scan cancelled.
issue_note: Cancellation is served by DELETE /discovery/jobs/:id (discovery.routes.ts:61); the CLI uses both a wrong path (/scans/:id/cancel) and a wrong method (POST), so it 404s.

user_story: As an operator, I want to schedule recurring discovery via cron so that scans run automatically.
expected_behavior: Builds {targets, _scanType, _schedule} from --targets/--type/--cron (default '0 2 * * *') and POSTs to /discovery/schedule, then prints the returned scheduleId, cron and target count. Aborts locally if no targets supplied.
issue_note: The route's scheduleDiscoverySchema (discovery.routes.ts:23) requires _provider (and an optional _config object); the CLI's targets/_scanType/_schedule keys are stripped by stripUnknown, leaving required _provider missing so the request 400s.

user_story: As a stakeholder, I want a CMDB summary so that I can see totals and breakdowns at a glance.
expected_behavior: Sends GET /analytics/dashboard and, unless --json, renders summary (total_cis, unique_types, unique_environments, total_relationships, recent_discoveries_24h) plus bar-chart breakdowns by type, status and environment from response.data.data.

user_story: As a stakeholder, I want CI counts grouped by type so that I can understand inventory composition.
expected_behavior: Sends GET /analytics/ci-counts and prints each ci_type with count, percentage of total and a proportional bar; supports --json for raw output.

user_story: As a stakeholder, I want CI counts grouped by environment so that I can see distribution across environments.
expected_behavior: Sends GET /analytics/ci-environments and prints each environment with count, percentage and a bar; supports --json.

user_story: As an auditor, I want a CI's change history so that I can review what changed and when.
expected_behavior: Requires --ci-id locally, then sends GET /analytics/change-history with params ci_id and limit and prints each change's type, timestamp, field, old/new value and changed_by; supports --json.
issue_note: changeHistorySchema (analytics.routes.ts:29) requires _ci_id (underscored); the CLI sends ci_id/limit which stripUnknown drops, leaving required _ci_id missing so the request 400s.

user_story: As a stakeholder, I want relationship statistics and top-connected CIs so that I can find hubs in the graph.
expected_behavior: Concurrently GETs /analytics/relationship-counts and /analytics/top-connected (params limit, direction) and prints per-type counts with bars and a top-connected table; supports --json.
issue_note: top-connected expects underscored _limit/_direction (analytics.routes.ts:24); the plain limit/direction params are stripped so --limit/--direction silently fall back to server defaults (10, both).

user_story: As an analyst, I want to run named analytics queries so that I can get discovery stats, timelines, top-connected and dependency-depth reports.
expected_behavior: Maps queryType to an /analytics/* endpoint (rejecting unknown types locally), passes type-appropriate params, and formats results per type (summary+by_provider, timeline bars, top-connected list, dependency-depth distribution); supports --json.
issue_note: The endpoints exist, but the CLI sends plain params (start_date/end_date/interval/limit/direction) while the schemas expect underscored equivalents, so date/interval/limit/direction filters are stripped and defaults apply.

user_story: As an operator, I want data mart health and statistics so that I can confirm Neo4j-to-PostgreSQL sync is healthy.
expected_behavior: Concurrently GETs /datamart/health and /datamart/stats and prints colorized health (status, neo4j, postgres, lastSync) plus stats (CI counts, relationship count, last ETL duration, sync lag); supports --json.
issue_note: No /datamart routes are mounted in the REST server (server.ts has no /api/v1/datamart), so /datamart/health and /datamart/stats 404. The entire datamart status/sync/reconcile/refresh/validate surface has no backend.

user_story: As an operator, I want to trigger an ETL sync from Neo4j to PostgreSQL so that the data mart is refreshed.
expected_behavior: POSTs {batchSize, fullRefresh:!incremental, incrementalSince?, ciTypes?} to /datamart/sync, prints the returned jobId and config echo, and when --wait polls /jobs/:id every 5s (up to ~10min) for completion, printing results or failure.
issue_note: No POST /datamart/sync route exists in the server (no /datamart mount in server.ts), so the trigger 404s; the --wait polling also depends on a single-segment /jobs/:id route that the jobs API does not provide (see CLI-035).

user_story: As an operator, I want to reconcile Neo4j and PostgreSQL so that data inconsistencies are detected/resolved.
expected_behavior: POSTs {conflictStrategy, autoResolve, maxAgeHours, ciIds?} to /datamart/reconcile with --strategy (neo4j-wins/postgres-wins/newest-wins/manual), prints the jobId and scope, and optionally --wait polls for completion.
issue_note: No POST /datamart/reconcile route is mounted (no /datamart group in server.ts), so the call 404s.

user_story: As an operator, I want a full data mart refresh so that I can completely resync after drift.
expected_behavior: Requires --confirm (otherwise prints a warning and returns), then POSTs {batchSize, fullRefresh:true, ciTypes?} to /datamart/sync, prints the jobId, and optionally --wait polls for completion.
issue_note: Targets the same missing /datamart/sync endpoint as CLI-031, so it 404s once --confirm is supplied.

user_story: As an operator, I want to validate integrity between Neo4j and PostgreSQL so that I can trust the data mart.
expected_behavior: Sends GET /datamart/validate with checkCounts/checkRelationships/checkOrphans/sampleSize params and prints overall status, record-count diffs, relationship integrity, orphan records, and any issues/recommendations; supports --json.
issue_note: No GET /datamart/validate route is mounted (no /datamart group in server.ts), so the call 404s.

user_story: As an operator, I want to check an ETL job's status so that I can monitor a triggered sync.
expected_behavior: Sends GET /jobs/:jobId and prints BullMQ-style fields: id, name, state, progress, data config, returnvalue results, failedReason, and timestamps; supports --json.
issue_note: The jobs API requires a queue segment: GET /jobs/:queueName/:jobId (jobs.routes.ts:60). A single-segment GET /jobs/:jobId instead matches GET /jobs/:queueName (listJobs) with queueName set to the jobId, returning the wrong handler/result.

user_story: As an operator, I want to list recent ETL jobs so that I can review queue activity.
expected_behavior: Sends GET /jobs with params limit and optional status and prints a table of job id/name/state/created from an array response; supports --json.
issue_note: There is no bare GET /jobs route; all jobs routes require at least a queue segment (e.g. GET /jobs/:queueName, jobs.routes.ts:63), so GET /jobs 404s.

user_story: As an operator, I want to start discovery/etl workers (and optionally schedulers) so that background jobs are processed.
expected_behavior: For type discovery/etl/all it calls the corresponding worker manager start() (and, with -s/--schedulers, the scheduler start()), installs SIGINT/SIGTERM handlers to stay resident, and exits 1 on an unknown type. Operates in-process via @cmdb/discovery-engine and @cmdb/etl-processor, not the REST API.
issue_note: createWorkerCommand() (worker.command.ts) is never imported or registered in index.ts (registerCommands wires only discovery/ci/query/analytics/datamart/connectors/config/health), so the entire 'worker' group is dead code and 'cmdb worker' is an unknown command.

user_story: As an operator, I want to stop discovery/etl workers and their schedulers so that I can halt background processing.
expected_behavior: For type discovery/etl/all it calls the matching worker manager stop() and scheduler stop(), reports success and exits 0. In-process operation via the engine packages.
issue_note: Unreachable because the worker group is not registered in index.ts (see CLI-037).

user_story: As an operator, I want to see worker status so that I know what is running.
expected_behavior: Calls getAllWorkerStatuses() on the discovery and ETL worker managers and prints discovery worker name/running/concurrency and ETL healthy/running flags. In-process operation.
issue_note: Unreachable because the worker group is not registered in index.ts (see CLI-037).

user_story: As an operator, I want to pause a specific worker so that I can temporarily stop it without shutting everything down.
expected_behavior: Intended to pause the named worker; currently logs 'Pause worker functionality not yet implemented' and prints a 'coming soon' message, then exits 0 without changing state.
issue_note: Explicit unimplemented stub (worker.command.ts:158-160): the command is a no-op with a TODO to add pauseWorker; it never pauses anything (and the group is also unregistered, see CLI-037).

user_story: As an operator, I want to resume a paused worker so that processing continues.
expected_behavior: Intended to resume the named worker; currently logs 'Resume worker functionality not yet implemented' and prints a 'coming soon' message, then exits 0 without changing state.
issue_note: Explicit unimplemented stub (worker.command.ts:174-176): the command is a no-op with a TODO to add resumeWorker (and the group is also unregistered, see CLI-037).

user_story: As an operator, I want to list jobs in a queue by state so that I can inspect the BullMQ queues.
expected_behavior: With no queueName it lists the QUEUE_NAMES; with a queueName it fetches waiting/active/completed/failed jobs (default waiting, limit 20) via the queue manager and prints id/name/state/timestamps/progress/failedReason. Operates in-process via @cmdb/common getQueueManager(), not REST.
issue_note: createJobsCommand() (jobs.command.ts) is never imported or registered in index.ts, so the entire 'jobs' group is dead code and 'cmdb jobs' is an unknown command.

user_story: As an operator, I want to trigger a discovery job immediately for a provider so that I can run ad-hoc discovery.
expected_behavior: Parses optional --config JSON and calls getDiscoveryScheduler().triggerDiscovery(provider, config, 'cli'), printing the queued jobId. Supports providers aws/azure/gcp/ssh/nmap. In-process operation.
issue_note: Unreachable because the jobs group is not registered in index.ts (see CLI-042).

user_story: As an operator, I want to trigger an ETL job immediately so that I can run sync/change-detection/reconciliation/full-refresh on demand.
expected_behavior: Parses optional --config JSON and calls getETLScheduler().triggerETL(type, config, 'cli'), printing the queued jobId. In-process operation.
issue_note: Unreachable because the jobs group is not registered in index.ts (see CLI-042).

user_story: As an operator, I want a job's status and progress so that I can track its execution.
expected_behavior: Calls queueManager.getJob(queueName, jobId); if missing, errors and exits 1; otherwise prints queue, name, state, timestamps, duration, progress, returnvalue, failedReason and stacktrace. In-process operation.
issue_note: Unreachable because the jobs group is not registered in index.ts (see CLI-042).

user_story: As an operator, I want to retry a failed job so that I can recover from transient failures.
expected_behavior: Calls queueManager.retryJob(queueName, jobId), logs success and exits 0. In-process operation.
issue_note: Unreachable because the jobs group is not registered in index.ts (see CLI-042).

user_story: As an operator, I want to cancel a job so that I can remove unwanted work from a queue.
expected_behavior: Calls queueManager.removeJob(queueName, jobId), logs success and exits 0. In-process operation.
issue_note: Unreachable because the jobs group is not registered in index.ts (see CLI-042).

user_story: As an operator, I want queue statistics so that I can see waiting/active/completed/failed/delayed counts.
expected_behavior: With a queueName prints that queue's waiting/active/completed/failed/delayed counts; without one iterates all QUEUE_NAMES printing per-queue counts (catching per-queue errors). In-process via getQueueManager().getQueueStats().
issue_note: Unreachable because the jobs group is not registered in index.ts (see CLI-042).

user_story: As an operator, I want to list available connectors from the registry so that I can see what I can install.
expected_behavior: GETs /connectors/registry (or /connectors/installed with --installed) passing category (upper-cased), search and verifiedOnly params, then renders a connector table. This group registers first, so it is the reachable 'connector' command.

user_story: As an operator, I want to list installed connectors so that I can see what is available locally.
expected_behavior: GETs /connectors/installed with optional category (upper-cased) and enabled params and renders an installed-connectors table, with guidance to install when none are found.

user_story: As an operator, I want detailed connector information so that I can review versions, resources, capabilities and stats.
expected_behavior: GETs /connectors/installed/:type by default or /connectors/registry/:type with --registry, then prints type, name, category, description, version info, resources, capabilities, run statistics, tags and install dates as applicable.

user_story: As an operator, I want to search the connector registry so that I can find connectors by name/description.
expected_behavior: GETs /connectors/registry with search=query plus optional category and verifiedOnly, then renders a connector table or a 'no matches' message.

user_story: As an operator, I want to install a connector from the registry so that I can use it for discovery/ingest.
expected_behavior: POSTs {connectorType, version?, skipDependencies?} to /connectors/install and, on success, prints installed type/name/version/path, lists resources, and suggests next steps; on failure prints the message/errors.
issue_note: ConnectorInstallCommand.register adds a second top-level program.command('connector'), but ConnectorListCommand's 'connector' is registered first in index.ts (lines 86 then 89). Commander resolves the first match, which has no 'install' subcommand, so 'cmdb connector install' (and the whole install/update/uninstall/outdated/verify/repair/cache group) is an unknown command.

user_story: As an operator, I want to update a connector (or all connectors) so that I run the latest version.
expected_behavior: With a type it PUTs /connectors/:type/update (optionally --version); with --all it fetches installed connectors and PUT-updates each that is outdated, printing an updated/skipped/failed summary. Errors locally if neither type nor --all is given.
issue_note: Unreachable: shadowed by the first-registered connector 'connector' command (see CLI-053).

user_story: As an operator, I want to uninstall a connector so that I can remove unused integrations.
expected_behavior: Without -f/--force it warns and returns; with --force it DELETEs /connectors/:type and reports success or the failure message.
issue_note: Unreachable: shadowed by the first-registered connector 'connector' command (see CLI-053).

user_story: As an operator, I want to see which connectors have updates so that I can plan upgrades.
expected_behavior: GETs /connectors/installed, filters entries where installed version differs from latest available, and prints a table of type/current/latest or 'All connectors are up to date'.
issue_note: Unreachable: shadowed by the first-registered connector 'connector' command (see CLI-053).

user_story: As an operator, I want to verify a connector's installation integrity so that I can confirm it is healthy.
expected_behavior: POSTs /connectors/:type/verify and reports success ('All checks passed') or lists the verification errors.
issue_note: Unreachable: shadowed by the first-registered connector 'connector' command (see CLI-053).

user_story: As an operator, I want to repair a broken connector installation so that I can recover it without reinstalling.
expected_behavior: POSTs /connectors/:type/repair and reports success or the failure message.
issue_note: Unreachable: shadowed by the first-registered connector 'connector' command (see CLI-053). Note the server defines POST /connectors/:type/verify but no /:type/repair route, so even if reachable repair would 404.

user_story: As an operator, I want to refresh or clear the connector registry cache so that I get fresh catalog data.
expected_behavior: With --refresh it POSTs /connectors/registry/refresh (printing cached count); with --clear it DELETEs /connectors/registry/cache; errors locally if neither flag is set.
issue_note: Unreachable: shadowed by the first-registered connector 'connector' command (see CLI-053). Endpoints also mismatch: the server exposes POST /connectors/cache/refresh (not /registry/refresh) and has no DELETE /connectors/registry/cache.

user_story: As an operator, I want to create a connector configuration so that I can connect to a target system.
expected_behavior: In --non-interactive mode it requires --type/--name/--connection (JSON), POSTs {name, connectorType, connection, enabledResources?} to /connector-configs, and prints the new config id/name/type/enabled with next-step hints.
issue_note: ConnectorConfigCommand registers program.command('connector').command('config'), but ConnectorListCommand's 'connector' is registered first (index.ts:86) and has no 'config' subcommand, so 'cmdb connector config ...' is an unknown command (whole config group unreachable). Interactive mode is also a stub: without --non-interactive it just prints usage and returns (connector-config.command.ts:130-144).

user_story: As an operator, I want to list connector configurations so that I can see what is set up.
expected_behavior: GETs /connector-configs with optional connectorType and enabled filters and prints a table of name/type/enabled/schedule, with guidance to create one when none exist.
issue_note: Unreachable: 'connector config' is shadowed by the first-registered connector command (see CLI-060).

user_story: As an operator, I want to view a configuration's details so that I can audit its settings.
expected_behavior: Looks up the config by name via GET /connector-configs?name, then prints basic info, scheduling, connection (masking password/secret values), enabled resources, error-handling, notification and metadata fields; reports 'not found' when the lookup is empty.
issue_note: Unreachable: 'connector config' is shadowed by the first-registered connector command (see CLI-060).

user_story: As an operator, I want to edit a configuration so that I can update connection, schedule and enabled state.
expected_behavior: Resolves the config id by name, builds a partial update from --name/--description/--connection(JSON)/--enable/--disable/--schedule/--schedule-enable/--schedule-disable, aborts if nothing to change, then PUTs /connector-configs/:id and reports success.
issue_note: Unreachable: 'connector config' is shadowed by the first-registered connector command (see CLI-060).

user_story: As an operator, I want to delete a configuration with a confirmation guard so that I do not remove it accidentally.
expected_behavior: Without -f/--force it warns and returns; with --force it resolves the id by name and DELETEs /connector-configs/:id, reporting success or 'not found'.
issue_note: Unreachable: 'connector config' is shadowed by the first-registered connector command (see CLI-060).

user_story: As an operator, I want to test a configuration's connection so that I can confirm credentials work before running it.
expected_behavior: Resolves the id by name and POSTs /connector-configs/:id/test, then prints success with optional message/details or failure with the error list.
issue_note: Unreachable: 'connector config' is shadowed by the first-registered connector command (see CLI-060).

user_story: As an operator, I want to enable a configuration so that it can run.
expected_behavior: Resolves the id by name and POSTs /connector-configs/:id/enable, reporting the configuration enabled.
issue_note: Unreachable: 'connector config' is shadowed by the first-registered connector command (see CLI-060).

user_story: As an operator, I want to disable a configuration so that it stops running.
expected_behavior: Resolves the id by name and POSTs /connector-configs/:id/disable, reporting the configuration disabled.
issue_note: Unreachable: 'connector config' is shadowed by the first-registered connector command (see CLI-060).

user_story: As an operator, I want to list/add/remove enabled resources for a configuration so that I control what it ingests.
expected_behavior: Resolves the config by name; with --list it prints currently enabled resources; with --add/--remove it computes the new set (de-duplicated) and PUTs {enabledResources} to /connector-configs/:id/resources, reporting the resulting count.
issue_note: Unreachable: 'connector config' is shadowed by the first-registered connector command (see CLI-060).

user_story: As an operator, I want to run a connector configuration so that I can ingest data on demand.
expected_behavior: Looks up the config id by name (GET /connector-configs?name), POSTs {resourceId?} to /connector-configs/:id/run, prints run id/config/type/status/started, and with --wait polls /connector-runs/:id every 2s until completion/timeout.
issue_note: ConnectorRunCommand adds yet another top-level program.command('connector'); ConnectorListCommand's 'connector' is registered first (index.ts:86), so 'cmdb connector run' (and runs/run-status/cancel/metrics) resolves to the wrong command and is unknown. The --wait/run-status polling also targets /connector-runs/:id while the server serves runs at /connector-configs/runs/:runId.

user_story: As an operator, I want to list connector runs (optionally for one config) so that I can review run history.
expected_behavior: Optionally resolves a configId by name, then GETs /connector-runs with limit/configId?/status?(upper-cased)/connectorType? and renders a runs table (id/config/status/records/duration).
issue_note: Unreachable: shadowed by the first-registered connector command (see CLI-069). The server also exposes global runs at /connector-configs/runs/all, not /connector-runs.

user_story: As an operator, I want a run's status (optionally watched live) so that I can monitor execution.
expected_behavior: GETs /connector-runs/:id and prints basic info, timing, record counts (extracted/transformed/loaded/failed), execution metadata and any errors; with --watch it polls every 2s, printing status transitions and a final summary on completed/failed/cancelled.
issue_note: Unreachable: shadowed by the first-registered connector command (see CLI-069). The server serves a run at /connector-configs/runs/:runId, not /connector-runs/:id.

user_story: As an operator, I want to cancel a running connector job so that I can stop it.
expected_behavior: POSTs an empty body to /connector-runs/:id/cancel and reports the run cancelled.
issue_note: Unreachable: shadowed by the first-registered connector command (see CLI-069). The server serves cancellation at /connector-configs/runs/:runId/cancel.

user_story: As an operator, I want metrics for a connector configuration so that I can assess reliability and throughput.
expected_behavior: Resolves the config by name, then GETs /connector-configs/:id/metrics (or /connector-configs/:id/resources/:resourceId/metrics with --resource) and prints total/successful/failed runs, success rate, average duration, total records and per-resource metrics.
issue_note: Unreachable: shadowed by the first-registered connector command (see CLI-069).
